annotations.label: Category

anchor.snippet: consists of the following three mathematical entities

annotations.note: `class Category` bundles a `CategoryStruct` (objects, hom-sets, identities, composition) together with the associativity and unit axioms.

annotations.label: Category of sets

anchor.snippet: A prototypical example of a category is Set

annotations.note: `instance CategoryTheory.types : Category.{u} (Type u)` equips `Type u` with the category whose morphisms are functions.

annotations.label: Monomorphism, epimorphism, and related morphism properties

anchor.snippet: Morphisms can have any of the following properties

annotations.note: `Mono` and `Epi` are defined in `Category.Basic`, with `IsIso` (Iso.lean) and `SplitMono`/`SplitEpi`/`IsSplitMono`/`IsSplitEpi` (EpiMono.lean) covering sections and retractions.

annotations.label: Equivalent characterizations of isomorphisms

anchor.snippet: Every retraction is an epimorphism, and every section is a monomorphism

annotations.note: `SplitEpi.epi`/`IsSplitEpi.epi` (and the dual `SplitMono.mono`) in `EpiMono.lean` give the stated facts that retractions are epi and sections are mono.

annotations.label: Covariant functor

anchor.snippet: covariant ) functor F from a category C to a category D

annotations.note: `structure Functor (C) [Category C] (D) [Category D]` packages object/morphism maps with `map_id` and `map_comp`.

annotations.label: Contravariant functor

anchor.snippet: A contravariant functor F : C → D is like a covariant functor

annotations.note: Mathlib models contravariant functors as covariant functors `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ D`, with `Functor.op`/`leftOp`/`rightOp` in `Opposites.lean` providing the conversions.

annotations.label: Natural transformation

anchor.snippet: then a natural transformation η from F to G associates to every object

annotations.note: `structure NatTrans (F G : C ⥤ D)` provides components `app` and the naturality square `naturality`.

annotations.label: Naturally isomorphic functors

anchor.snippet: The two functors F and G are called naturally isomorphic

annotations.note: Natural isomorphisms are `Iso` in the functor category; the `NatIso` namespace collects the API including componentwise `IsIso` characterizations.

annotations.label: Functor category

anchor.snippet: The functor category D C has as objects the functors from C to D

annotations.note: `instance Functor.category : Category (C ⥤ D)` gives the category whose objects are functors and morphisms are natural transformations.

annotations.label: Strict 2-category

anchor.snippet: a (strict) 2-category is a category together with "morphisms between morphisms"

annotations.note: `class Bicategory.Strict` asserts the associators/unitors of a `Bicategory` are identities, yielding a strict 2-category.
